reasoning: As of October 2025, the highest reported single-run score on the FrontierMath benchmark is 29%, achieved by GPT-5 with a 10x token budget (search_1_result_1). The benchmark creators at Epoch AI explicitly stated that less than 70% of the questions are even "within reach" for today's top models under maximum test-time compute conditions, and they project 70% accuracy might arrive in the first half of 2026 at the earliest (search_4_result_1). Human experts score in the 30-50% range, making this an exceptionally difficult benchmark. Furthermore, test-time compute scaling shows diminishing returns, adding only ~1% absolute improvement per doubling of compute, meaning an 80% score cannot simply be brute-forced before the end of the year. With the forecast date in November 2025 and the SOTA standing ~50 percentage points away from the target, achieving 80% by December 31, 2025 is virtually impossible. I am setting my prediction to 0.05, aligning with the market estimate of ~4% and the lowest bounds of reasonable possibility.
